title: Ask-n-Learn: Active Learning via Reliable Gradient Representations for Image Classification
summary: This paper introduces Ask-n-Learn, an active learning method designed to overcome limitations in existing techniques. It addresses confirmation bias in pseudo-labeling methods and the poor performance of methods using uncalibrated uncertainty scores. The proposed approach uses calibrated uncertainties to generate reliable gradient embeddings for sample selection and incorporates a data augmentation strategy to mitigate confirmation bias.

methodology.core_technique: Active learning using gradient embeddings for sample selection.
key_innovations: Utilizing calibrated uncertainties to produce reliable gradient embeddings.
Employing a data augmentation strategy during pseudo-labeling to avoid confirmation bias.

contributions: A new active learning framework, Ask-n-Learn, that integrates calibrated uncertainties and data augmentation to improve sample selection.
A method for generating more reliable gradient embeddings, which better balance the exploitation-exploration trade-off in active learning.
baselines: Methods based on uncertainty scores (e.g., entropy)
results: The proposed Ask-n-Learn approach significantly outperforms existing active learning approaches on benchmark image classification tasks (SVHN, MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10) using various deep architectures.
